summary: Add the Opportunity Analysis Agent with typed schemas, docs-aligned prompt construction, injected safe JSON completion, deterministic heuristic fallback, and risk-policy guardrails.
acceptance_criteria: A reusable Opportunity Analysis Agent module exists under agents/ with typed input, output, tool concept, monetization, risk, score hint, Zod schemas, and a stable public function for analyzing an opportunity from a Radar task, keyword candidate, SERP analysis, and SERP results.
The agent builds a prompt aligned with docs/08_ai_agents_and_prompts.md Agent 3 and calls the existing safeJsonCompletion() utility through an injected LlmClient when one is provided.

The agent provides a deterministic heuristic fallback that creates a structured opportunity from prior agent outputs without external API keys, process.env reads, network calls, database writes, or paid services.

The output includes title, summary, targetUser, searchIntent, recommendedToolType, toolConcept, monetization, risk, buildComplexity, scoreHints, and killCriteria, with score hint values clamped to 0-100.

Risk guardrails prevent promises about revenue, rankings, legal compliance, medical outcomes, financial outcomes, or guaranteed business results; legal, tax, medical, and financial topics are constrained to checklist/self-assessment framing or high/excluded risk; adult, gambling, gray-market, or harmful topics are excluded.

Focused automated tests cover LLM success, malformed or rejected LLM output falling back to heuristics, score hint clamping/normalization, risk guardrails, monetization/user-fit inference, and deterministic fallback output.
README documents the Opportunity Analysis Agent slice, heuristic fallback behavior, risk guardrails, no-secret/no-network/no-persistence policy, and the test command.
No scoring engine, scan orchestration, database persistence, API route, UI, auth, cron, deployment, real LLM provider wiring, SERP provider changes, or paid service setup is implemented.
notes: Derived from docs/08_ai_agents_and_prompts.md Agent 3 prompt/schema and docs/11_development_plan.md M3 agent foundation.

docs/09_codex_implementation_tasks.md mentions saving Opportunity for Task 9; this smaller M3 slice deliberately defers persistence to the later scan orchestrator/API task to keep the agent foundation verifiable and side-effect free.
systems_architect is required because this extends the agents/ boundary and feeds later scoring and scan orchestration work.
domain_designer is required because this task owns opportunity framing, monetization language, risk policy, and no-guarantee product claims.
Keep scoreHints as structured LLM or fallback hints only. The final calculateOpportunityScore() function belongs to M4.
non_goals: No scoring engine, calculateOpportunityScore(), score breakdown persistence, MVP Spec Agent, or scan orchestrator.
No Opportunity database writes and no Prisma/lib changes.
No /api/scans/run route, dashboard integration, opportunity list/detail UI, or status update UI.
No real LLM provider configuration, environment-variable secret loading, real network calls in tests, or paid service setup.
No changes to services/llm, services/serp, or existing SERP Analysis Agent contracts unless required for type-only imports from public exports.
